Xi Jinping:
Biography of Xi Jinping

Birth and Early Life (1953–1975):
Xi Jinping was born in 1953 in Beijing. His father Xi Jinping’s father Xi Jinping’s father Xi Jinping’s father Xi Jinping’s father Xi Jinping’s father Xi Jinping’s father Xi Jinping’s father Xi Jinping’s father Xi Jinping’s father Xi Jinping’s father Xi Jinping, who was a politician, and his mother was a teacher. In his childhood life he was made aware of education and politics under the Communist Party of China. In 1969 he was sent to an agricultural area in Shaanxi province, where he lived life with farmers, which shows an important role in rural development and people of society in his political outlook.

Education and Political Start (1975–1980):
In 1975 he obtained a degree in chemistry at Tsinghua University in Beijing. After that he took part in the Communist Party youth writer association, where his political skills developed. In 1980 he was appointed as a political officer in Fujian province, where he worked for economic and social development.

Rising Political Opportunities (1980–2002):
In the 1980s he was appointed as a political officer in Jiangxi province, where he played an important role in the development of economic policies. In the 1990s he was appointed as a political officer in Shanghai, where he worked for urban development and economic expansion. In 2002 he was appointed as provincial CEO of Jiangxi, where his leadership played an important role in economic development.

Top Leadership of Communist Party (2002–2013):
In 2002 he was appointed as General Secretary of the Communist Party of China, which was an important stage for playing the role of top political leadership. Under his leadership, China made an important change in economic development, internal security and international relations. In 2007 he was appointed as President of China, which shows the top stage of his leadership.

Leadership and Policies (2013 to present):
Under Xi Jinping’s leadership, China under the "Chinese Dream" made an important change in economic development, technological innovation and international relations with the goal of national rejuvenation. His policies especially include the "Belt and Road Initiative" and under internal development a new development plan with the goal of technological autonomy played an important role. Under his leadership, China made an important change in internal development and international relations, which shows the goal of his leadership.

Conclusion:
Xi Jinping’s biography is a long journey to play an important role as a political leader, which shows an important change in China’s economic development, national rejuvenation and international relations. Under his leadership, China played an important role under a new development plan, which shows the goal of his leadership.
Barack Obama:
Biography of Barack Obama

Birth and Early Life (1961–1971):
Barack Hussein Obama II (Barack Hussein Obama II) was born in 1961 in Oho City of Hawaii. His father, Barack Hussein Obama Jr. (Barack Hussein Obama Sr.), was a Kenyan politician, while his mother, Mary Obama (Mary Ann Dunham), was an American actress. Obama’s father was taken to Kenya, where he spent most of his childhood. His mother took him with her to Indonesia, where he spent most of his childhood.

Education and Employment (1971–1990):
Obama obtained a bachelor’s degree in political science from Columbia University, then obtained a legal degree from Harvard University’s legal university. He worked in Chicago as a social reformer, where he worked for poor and minority groups. During his employment he developed his views on various issues of American politics and society.

Entry into Politics (1990–2004):
Obama worked as a judicial advocate in Chicago and later worked as a private advocate. In 1996 he ran for election as a member of the Chicago city state assembly, where he began his political career. In 2004 he won the election as a member of the Chicago city state assembly, where his speech played an important role in his political leadership.

As President of the United States (2008–2017):
Obama won the 2008 United States presidential election, which was the first time in American history that an African-American became president. During his presidency he made an important contribution to his leadership as President of the United States, including support for African-American rights, development of the American economy, and direction of international relations.

Major Political Achievements:
Support for African-American rights: Obama is known for his leadership in supporting African-American rights.
Development of the American economy: During his presidency he is known for policies on development of the American economy and various economic issues.
Direction of international relations: Obama is known for leadership in directing international relations, including relations between Africa, America and other countries.

Retirement and Present (until 2017 writing):
After the end of his term as President of the United States in 2017, Obama returned to university life after the end of his political career. He worked as a regular legal teacher at Harvard University, where he shared his ideas and experiences with students.

Nobel Peace Prize (2009):
Obama won the Nobel Peace Prize in 2009, which became an important achievement for his leadership as President of the United States during his presidency.

Conclusion:
The biography of Barack Obama is an important study of his leadership as President of the United States, known for support of African-American rights, development of the American economy and direction of international relations. During his presidency he is known in the history of America as a new study of leadership.
Dilma Rousseff:
Biography of Dilma Rousseff

Birth and Early Life
Dilma Rousseff (Dilma Rousseff) was born on 13 January 1943 in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. Her father was a worker, while her mother was a teacher. Her family’s economic situation was weak, due to which she carefully chose the stages of her life so as not to waste her educational opportunities. She obtained a doctorate degree in economics from the National University of Brazil (UFRJ).

Beginning of Political Career
In the 1980s she joined the Brazilian Workers’ Party (PT) and began making her mark in politics. She worked at the state level as Minister of Education and Social Development, where she played an important role in developing policies for poverty and education.

As Prime Minister and President

As Minister (2003–2010): She worked under the leadership of Bernardo Cobos as the Minister of National Development, where she made important contributions to the expansion of social security programs such as Bolsa Família.
President (2011–2016): In 2010 she was elected as President of Brazil, becoming Brazil’s first female president. During her rule she was nominated for the Nobel Prize for international equality for her contributions to Brazil’s economic development and social policies.

Economic Challenges and Divorce
In 2015, amid Brazil’s economic crisis, accusations were made against Rousseff’s government, including allegations of indirect financial crimes. In 2016 she was divorced, after which she was sent to jail. There is ongoing debate about the allegations against her, including the seriousness of the accusations and the difference between political motives.

Response and Opposition
After the allegations against Rousseff, widespread opposition arose, where her supporters viewed the accusations as political opposition. International authorities have warned about the allegations against her, including uncertainty about the basis of the accusations.
